mnemonics.title: DocumentListener Methods
mnemonics.content: Insert Remove Changed - IRC for InsertUpdate, RemoveUpdate, ChangedUpdate - remember the order matters!

mnemonics.title: JTextField Features
mnemonics.content: S.E.A.R - SetColumns for size, Editable for read-only, ActionListener for Enter key, getText/getDocument for retrieval

acronyms.usage: JTextField implements MVC: Model is PlainDocument (stores text), View is UI delegate (renders text), Controller handles events and user interactions.
The umbrella that includes Swing components like JTextField. Part of javax.swing package.
The predecessor to Swing. Swing components like JTextField are built on AWT but provide more features.

visualTips.visualization: Draw a tree: JComponent → JTextComponent → JTextField → JPasswordField. This shows inheritance and what methods are available at each level.
User Input → DocumentFilter.insertString() → Document → UI Update. Show the filter as a gatekeeper controlling what enters the document.
For JTextField: KeyType → DocumentListener (real-time), EnterKey → ActionListener, FocusLost → FocusListener. Three different triggers for three scenarios.

analogies.analogy: Think of JTextField like a document in a folder. The Document is the folder, the text is the paper inside, and the JTextField is just the window showing it.
DocumentFilter is like a bouncer at a club - it checks each character trying to enter and either lets it in, transforms it, or rejects it before it reaches the document.
JTextField is like a single-line input box on a form; JTextArea is like a comment box that accepts multiple lines of text.
